creator: diizzy@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jason E. Hale from comment #7)
Quirk is probably the wrong word for it, "hidden" feature is probably better. Given the current status it's very easy to overlook let alone find it as Google or whatever search engine you perfer won't return something usable. Telling people to "by default" look at the source code is neither helpful, inviting or productive.

My point is that if the goal is make it tedious for people to utilize ports framework "properly" we're on the wrong path.

If we look at the fallout there's quite a high amount (given the amount of ports utilizing it in tree) that gets this wrong and I'd argue that if we only had one way defining the number would be much lower.

Your mentioned examples also shows a pattern worth looking into,

Looking at USE_LOCALE there seems to be ~30% or so of all ports doing something else than utilizing USE_LOCALE.

USE_BINUTILS shows about 50% or possibly even more not using it in favour of BUILD_DEPENDS

I'll try to wrap up the Part 2/3 later today (3/3 being the list already posted) and then tackle CSTD as time allows.
